gates.note: Every load-bearing assumption is sourced and argued: PSG FY2030 revenue base $4.59B traced to Theia demand model, TrendForce 22-25% SiC share, YOLE/Mordor $6.5B market; WACC 10.5% built via CAPM with explicit fundamental beta de-risking from 1.977 regression to 1.6 (credited contracted LTAs + EU grant); Gordon exit 13.67x computed from r=10.5%, g=4.5%, ROIC=25% -- not picked; AMG Treo path anchored on company-stated $1B FY2030 target + Q1 2026 2.5x QoQ observed ramp; ISG 16x EV/EBIT from Sony 22-25x and OmniVision 14-18x comp range. The $1.19B non-GAAP addback is sourced to Q1 2026 8-K GAAP vs non-GAAP actuals. Infineon bear mechanism cited to company-filed press releases (February 2025 Villach rollout, November 2024 Stellantis agreement). China localization sourced to S&P Global Automotive Insights September 2025 and STMicro earnings call statements. No bare numbers found.

Daedalus bias_audit is present in ON_valuation.json and ON.json. 9 assumptions reviewed: 1 SLIGHT-CONSERVATIVE (AMG EBIT margin 19-20% vs possible honest-center 22-24%), 8 NEUTRAL, 0 AGGRESSIVE. same_direction_count=1, well below the 3+ threshold. Erinys independently verified the tally. The very large discount vs price ($45.53 vs $121.62) is explicitly defended as a finding -- the result of NOPAT-based valuation vs the street's non-GAAP EPS convention, not a conservative artifact. The bias audit correctly addresses the most dangerous outcome of this SELL: that it could be a stacked-conservative false SELL. It is not.

onsemi is a three-segment conglomerate; SOTP is correctly applied per CONTRACT §2.1. PSG (high-growth SiC) uses ExitMultiple with Gordon steady-state formula at FY2030 normalization -- the most efficient method per CONTRACT §2.2. AMG (moderate-growth Treo + legacy analog) uses ExitMultiple at FY2030 -- appropriate for an early-stage embedded platform in a moderate-growth legacy base. ISG (mature-cyclical CMOS imaging) uses earnings multiple (EV/EBIT) -- correct for a near-steady-state business. Each segment has its own CAPM-derived WACC (PSG 10.5%, AMG 9.5%, ISG 9.5%) with explicit fundamental beta build shown. T-invariance verified over T=3 to T=5 (spread $0.71, 1.6%). The method_why is stated in both ON.json and ON_valuation.json.primary_method.reasoning. Mandatory sensitivity matrix present; dominant lever named (PSG FY2030 revenue >> EBIT margin). Sensitivity axis label transposition (Erinys cosmetic flag) does not affect the numbers.

All four levers are present and argued in ON.json.four_levers: (1) Growth rate and duration -- PSG 10% CAGR FY2026-2030 base, T-invariance confirms FY2030 as genuine normalization year, level of revenue at normalization (base $4.59B vs bull $7.08B vs bear $2.65B PSG) identified as the dominant swing. (2) Economics -- margins argued from Czech fab utilization trajectory; owner FCF $2.64/share computed from NOPAT $1.17B company-level less SBC. (3) WACC -- CAPM build shown for all three segments; fundamental beta rationale explicit for each. (4) Exit multiple -- Gordon formula output shown with inputs, reconciled to peer EV/EBIT range. The earnings bridge (ON.json.earnings_bridge) ties GAAP EPS, non-GAAP EPS, and owner FCF together, completing the numerator bridge. Sensitivity matrix identifies revenue as the dominant lever and ROIC as second-order.

The crux is named in ON.json.our_view (the opening paragraph), in ON.json.crux.one_line, and in ON.json.thesis[0] (The denominator gap is the call). It is stated as: the market values onsemi on 28x non-GAAP EPS that adds back $1.19B/year of real costs; owner FCF is $2.64/share; the SOTP on NOPAT yields $45.53. The crux is resolved in key_debates[0] (the denominator debate) with a clear verdict: bear view wins; D&A on the SiC fab is a real cost; the justified multiple on owner FCF is 15-18x implying ~$45/share. The report also correctly names the ONE trigger that would flip the call: if onsemi demonstrates three consecutive years of capex materially below D&A, the addback would be partially valid. Section §1 (our_view) names the crux; §7 (key_debates[0]) resolves it. PASS.

Three key debates present, each with bull view, bear view, our verdict, what_would_change_our_mind, and worth_per_share. Debate 1 (denominator/crux): worth $76/share at current price, confirmed as the single largest swing. Debate 2 (SiC share trajectory): +$12.93/sh bull, -$14.80/sh bear, range $28-30/sh around base. Debate 3 (AI datacenter GaNEXUS): +$10.56/sh bull, -$6-8/sh bear. Each debate has an evidence_quality grade (EXTRAPOLATED / OBSERVABLE-AND-NEAR / MECHANISM-NOT-YET-FIRING). Debates are argued, not listed. Each includes the specific observable signal that would flip the verdict. The debates are the correct ones -- they anchor on the crux (denominator gap) and the two secondary contested variables (SiC share, AI datacenter). Note: Erinys flagged a minor inconsistency in Boreas's single-debate SiC-share delta ($12.93 stated vs $10.10 computed from stated NOPAT increment) -- but this is internal to Boreas's quantification text and the full bull $80.15 is verified correct. The debates as presented in ON.json.key_debates are coherent and pass.

The report follows the 8-section arc per CONTRACT §6: (1) our_view = the call with the four numbers and crux named; (2) business_overview = segments, business model, historical trend read as a narrative (not a transcribed table); (3) industry_position = market overview, named competitors with share dynamics, five forces, moat; (4) market_view = reverse-multiple decomposition, analyst tape with 7 named post-Q1 actions, sentiment narrative; (5) management = CEO, CFO, capital allocation record; (6) valuation_method + segment_valuation + four_levers = the argued base; (7) key_debates = 3 debates argued both ways with verdicts; (8) range_and_sensitivity + scenarios + conclusion = the call restated with the range. Conclusion is present and conclusion-first. Prose is readable with a clear bear paragraph, bull/bear theses, and synthesis_paths. No 47-card dump -- the debates are 3 focused arguments, not a laundry list. The snapshot has 24 items (>15 threshold). The peers section is read as a narrative (peers_read). Historical financials have an interpreted read (historical_financials_read). PASS.

Erinys PASS WITH NOTES. SOTP arithmetic verified to <0.05% rounding tolerance. Four numbers bridge verified. Bull $80.15 and bear Cassandra $19.85 verified. Bias audit tally verified. T-invariance verified. Three advisory flags: (1) COSMETIC -- sensitivity matrix axis labels transposed in JSON metadata (rows/columns described in reverse); actual grid values are correct. (2) MINOR -- #3 roll-forward uses r=9.0% stated while blended WACC is 10.2%; difference in #3 is $0.54 (1.1%), below 2% materiality threshold. (3) MINOR -- Boreas single-debate SiC delta ($12.93 stated vs $10.10 computed from stated inputs); full bull $80.15 is independently correct. None of these affect the fair value, target, or rating.

snapshot has 24 metrics (threshold 15). All three segments documented with revenue, margin, nature, and WACC. Historical financials cover FY2022-2025 and are interpreted (historical_financials_read). industry_position includes moat, five forces, five named competitors with share data and trajectory. 3 key debates (within the 2-4 range), each with real bear view, what_flips, and $/share worth. Street reconstruction present: consensus $107.15 decomposed into implied non-GAAP EPS multiple, street thesis stated in one line, and the precise agree/disagree articulated. Earnings bridge present and ties NOPAT to owner FCF to non-GAAP EPS. scenario_assumptions present with per-case numbered rows. catalysts_to_watch in 4 thematic buckets with horizon, what_to_watch, and bull/bear tell. data_gaps field present and populated. sources field populated with 20 entries, tiered. Rating SELL consistent with -62.7% implied return vs -10% threshold. Band (low=$12.21 / high=$80.15) matches valuation scenarios. NOTE: score.py band_matches_scenarios reported FAIL because the script reads valuation_headline.band_low/band_high from ON.json, but ON.json does not carry a top-level valuation_headline key -- the band lives in ON_headline.json. The band data itself is correct and present; this is a scorecard script schema-mismatch, not a report defect.

advisory_notes.note: Sensitivity matrix axis labels in ON_valuation.json and ON.json are transposed in the JSON metadata (matrix_note and axis_1/axis_2 labels say rows=margin and columns=revenue, but the actual grid is arranged with rows=revenue and columns=margin). The nine cell values are arithmetically correct. Correct the metadata labels before rendering the Excel Multiple Maths tab to avoid reader confusion.

The #3 fair_value_12m roll-forward uses r=9.0% in bridge_logic, while the stated blended WACC is 10.2%. The resulting $0.54 difference in #3 ($49.63 vs $50.17) is 1.1% -- below the 2% materiality threshold and does not change the rating or target. Reconcile the stated roll-forward rate to the blended WACC in a future revision.

Boreas's single-debate SiC-share-only delta is stated as +$12.93/sh but the stated NOPAT increment ($0.429B) computes to +$10.10/sh. The full bull case $80.15 is independently verified correct and unaffected. The inconsistency is limited to the intermediate single-debate quantification in ON_bull.json.delta_to_base_by_debate. Correct in a future revision.

score.py band_matches_scenarios reports FAIL because the mechanical checker reads valuation_headline.band_low/band_high from ON.json (where that key does not exist), not from ON_headline.json (where band_low=12.21 and band_high=80.15 are present and match the scenario range). The data is correct; the script schema reference needs updating.

Street reconstruction in ON_headline.json has street_target fields as null (mean, mean_multiple, mean_implied_growth_pct, high, return_pct, n_analysts all null). The street consensus IS documented in ON.json.market_view.street_view and ON.json.street_reconstruction with full narrative. The null fields in ON_headline.json are a structural gap in the structured output but the information is present in the report. Low priority -- no material impact.
